summary: SPH4U 'Revolutions in Modern Physics' problems use special relativity. A moving clock runs slow: the proper interval Δt₀ is stretched to Δt = Δt₀/√(1 − v²/c²). The effect only matters as the speed nears c.
method: Identify the proper time Δt₀ — the interval measured in the frame where the two events happen at the same place.
Find the relative speed v as a fraction of c.
Compute the Lorentz factor γ = 1/√(1 − v²/c²).
Multiply: the dilated time is Δt = γ·Δt₀.
Check the limit — at small v, γ ≈ 1 and Δt ≈ Δt₀.

example.title: A clock moving at 0.6c
example.find: the time Δt measured by a stationary observer
